entry.title: How to Migrate Current-Year Acquired Assets in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Using Migration Cockpit?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are operating in an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud environment and are currently migrating assets into a newly created company code.

Using the Migration Cockpit (Migrate Your Data app), along with the migration objects:

  • Fixed Asset – Master Data
  • Fixed Asset – Posting

we have successfully migrated assets acquired prior to the current financial year by maintaining cumulative values (prior-period assets).

However, we are facing challenges when trying to migrate assets acquired during the current financial year. We are unable to identify the correct approach to handle these current-year acquisitions using the available migration objects.

Could you please guide us on the following:

  • What is the recommended approach to migrate current-year asset acquisitions in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
  • Should these be handled via migration objects or through manual/operational postings (e.g., asset acquisition postings)?
  • Are there any best practices or standard procedures to follow for such scenarios?

Any guidance or recommendations would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Siva Nagi Reddy

entry.title: SAP INSIDE TRACK SÃO JOSE DOS CAMPOS 2026
entry.content:
Um Pouco da Nossa História

O SAP Inside Track teve sua primeira edição em São José dos Campos em 2025 e, desde então, tem sido um marco importante para a comunidade SAP na região. O evento faz parte de uma série global presente em muitos países, sendo organizado por membros ativos da Comunidade SAP com o intuito de promover o compartilhamento de conhecimento e fortalecer conexões profissionais.

No Brasil, o SAP Inside Track já acumula mais de 15 anos de existência, passando por 11 cidades diferentes em 40 edições pelo país. Cidades como São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, São José dos Campo, São Leopoldo, Joinville, Campinas, Salvador, Ribeirão Preto, Belo Horizonte, Curitiba, Fortaleza e Recife.

O Que Esperar do Evento

O SAP Inside Track São José dos Campos é a maior oportunidade de aprender, compartilhar experiências e conhecer outros profissionais do mercado SAP. Teremos palestras técnicas, funcionais e de negócios, abordando as mais diversas soluções SAP, incluindo as inovações do SAP S/4HANA.

Além disso, este é o momento perfeito para expandir seu networking e conhecer pessoalmente grandes profissionais da Comunidade SAP e das redes sociais.

Compre aqui 

Organizadores:

@JCGodoi  - Julio Godoi

@felipesoares  - Felipe Soares Andrade

@RobertaFernande - Roberta Fernandes

 

Para mais informações, publicações, agenda e mais, acompanhe nossas redes socials:

LINKTREE SITSP

INSTAGRAM SITBR

LINKEDIN SITSJC

Use #sitsjc2026 hashtag para nos mencionar.

entry.title: Candidate Fraud Detection: Reducing Hiring Risks
entry.content:
But what’s “candidate fraud”? The term refers to intentional deception regarding identity, qualifications, employment history, or credentials during the hiring process. This is a serious issue that can compromise the integrity of your recruitment efforts.  

Do you want to know how SAP Fieldglass features can help you to mitigate risks regarding candidate fraud during your selection processes? Then let’s dive into a 5-blog series to see how these features can help you to identify red flags and make more assertive decisions! 

In this first blog, we’ll check some tips and tricks that can help you during the candidate selection process. In the next 4 blogs, we’ll cover the following stages of the hiring process: 

  • Before the interview 
  • Hiring process 
  • Offer and start 
  • Post-hiring  

Candidate Selection 

This is the first step in finding the right candidate. It requires patience, precision, and attention to detail when reviewing the information that candidates provide. In this initial phase, it’s crucial to follow security best practices, such as: 

  • Verifying candidate profiles to confirm authenticity of credentials and work history  
  • Using trusted sources to source candidates   
  • Reviewing applications for duplicates or profiles with inconsistent information  
  • Researching the candidate online and comparing with the information provided (on social media or by reverse image searching, for example) 

Now, let’s check some SAP Fieldglass features that can help you to detect fraudulent information and choose the best candidates. 

Security ID 

A Security ID is a unique code made up of numbers and/or letters. It helps guarantee that each applicant is unique and reduces the risk of duplicate or fraudulent profiles. Also, ensures that only authorized candidates can access and update their profiles, preventing unauthorized users from falsifying their data. 

Recognizing a worker through distinctive security credentials also lets you monitor a worker's background across multiple assignments and service agreements, providing insight into the duration of a worker's tenure. 

You can define a Security ID format as either manually defined or auto-generated. 

Custom Fields and Activity Items for Supplier Evaluation 

You can create Custom Fields and Activity Items for supplier evaluation, enhancing the process by enabling more detailed data collection and automated workflows. Here are some examples of how fields and items you can add to enhance your selection process: 

  • Enhanced Candidate Profiles: You can add custom fields to capture additional, pertinent information about candidates that might not be covered by standard fields. This can include details such as previous employment verification, educational qualifications, certifications, and references. 
  • Compliance Checks: You can add custom fields to track compliance documents such as work permits, licenses, or criminal background checks.  
  • Supplier Performance Monitoring: You can add activity items to track supplier evaluation to guarantee that the suppliers are providing legitimate and qualified candidates. Poorly performing suppliers who frequently submit fraudulent candidates can be identified and potentially removed from the supply chain. 

Questionnaires for Suppliers 

You can create a Questionnaire for suppliers to complete if you need more details from a job seeker. Suppliers may be required to attest through questionnaire responses that they’ve verified the worker's identity, background, and qualifications, making the supplier directly responsible for any fraudulent misrepresentations. 

Machine Learning Features for Candidate Analysis 

Besides the most well-known features above, SAP Fieldglass also offers Machine Learning features that can be used in the selection process. These features can be used to detect candidate fraud by analyzing resumes, identifying inconsistencies, and enabling predictive security in hiring decisions.  

In the Machine Learning Features for Candidate Analysis page, you can see all the features that can be used in this stage of the hiring process.   

Now that you’ve learned some features that can help in your candidate selection process, let’s see in our next blog how to explore techniques before the interview, so you get prepared and ready to detect possible red flags.  

 

 

For the purposes of this guide, “candidate fraud” means deliberate misrepresentation of identity, credentials, work history, or qualifications during recruitment. This guide is preventive and educational; it does not make legal determinations.

entry.title: 1H 2026 - Release highlights of SAP SuccessFactors Performance and Goals Management
entry.content:
Hi Community,

The 1H 2026 release is here!

A new set of enhancements has been released in the 1H 2026 release that has been eagerly awaited. The SAP SuccessFactors 1H 2026 Release includes some key features and enhancements, so let's take a closer look!

1- AI-Assisted Performance Insights Available in All Step Types:

Managers can now gain insights into employees' performance when they're involved in iterative or collaborative steps. Previously, they could only use this feature in single-role steps.

This enhancement benefits managers by providing consistent access to AI-assisted performance insights across all review step types, improving the efficiency and effectiveness of performance evaluations.

      Enablement: Contact Customer Engagement Executive or Account Manager

      Configuration Requirements:

  • You've selected the Performance Management Feature Settings>Enable the latest experience of Performance Management forms option.
  • You've purchased the AI units license. For more information about AI units license, please contact your Account Executive.
  • You've consented to the AI Usage Acknowledgment Statement.
  • You've selected the AI Services Administration>Assisted Performance Insights option.
  • You've selected the Form Template Settings>Enable Assisted Performance Insights for Manager Review option for the intended form template.

2- Business Rule Enhancements on Configuring Dynamic Content for Latest Performance Management Forms:

The Change Field Visibility, Editability, and Required Status on Forms rule scenario has been renamed to Configure Dynamic Form Content, and incorporated with more functionality, such as setting field values, limiting rating field options, changing section visibility, and using the Get Count of Ratings function.

In the If section of the business rule, you can now define conditions using more fields and the Get Count of Ratings rule function.

These enhancements offer greater flexibility for modifying form content based on user input, enabling you to automate a wider range of scenarios in performance reviews.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

Using this rule scenario requires the following configurations. For step-by-step procedures and more information on the new use cases, see Creating a Business Rule to Change Field Visibility, Editability, and Required Status on Forms.

  • You've selected the following options in Performance Management Feature Settings:
    • Enable the latest experience of Performance Management forms
    • Enable business rules for Performance Management
  • In the form template, you've properly configured the fields or sections for which you want to create a rule.
  • You've used the Configure Dynamic Form Content rule scenario to create a business rule for the form template.
  • You've assigned the rule to the Form Opening and Form Saving triggers, and to the Field Change trigger as needed.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • Administrator Permissions>Manage System Properties>Performance Management Feature Settings
  • Administrator Permissions>Manage Templates>Form Templates and Comprehensive template configuration for PMv12
  • Administrator Permissions>Metadata Framework>Configure Business Rules
  • Administrator Permissions>Assign Rules in Talent>Assign Rules for Performance Management

3- Configure Goal Statuses That Don't Generate Home Page Cards:

In Admin Center>Manage Templates, we introduced a new configuration setting named Do Not Generate Cards on Home Page for Goal Management.

With this setting, you can choose which goal state values will not generate cards on the home page. As critical follow-up actions, you must save the template and choose the Refresh button. This is to make the setting effective and to update existing goals with those statuses so they no longer appear on the home page.

To prevent configuration conflicts, the same state value can't be selected for both Do Not Generate Cards on Home Page and Trigger Completion. If they overlap, you'll be prompted to adjust the selections before saving.

Previously, it wasn't possible to exclude specific statuses from generating cards, meaning statuses like Cancelled or Postponed could still generate cards and distract users.

Enablement: Customer Configured

Configuration Requirements:

Apart from the configuration setting in Manage Templates, you can also set no-generate-card="true" to one or more state values in the goal plan template to complete the configuration.

4- Default Expanded or Collapsed State for Section Descriptions on Latest Performance Management Forms:

With the latest experience of Performance Management forms, you can now set section descriptions to be expanded or collapsed by default. The section description area has also been improved for better accessibility.

In the Form Template Settings admin tool, there's a new option called Expand Form Section Descriptions on First Opening. By default, this setting is disabled, meaning section descriptions are collapsed when users open a form for the first time. If enabled, section descriptions will be expanded by default on first opening. Once a user manually expands or collapses a description, their preference is saved in their browser and remains the same when they reopen the form.

Enablement: Customer Configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • You've selected Enable the latest experience of Performance Management forms in the Performance Management Feature Settings admin tool.
  • If you want section descriptions to be expanded by default, make sure you've selected Expand Form Section Descriptions on First Opening in the Form Template Settings admin tool.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

You have the Administrator>Manage Templates>Form Templates permission.

5- Enhanced Mass Routing Functionality in Performance and 360 Form Inbox:

From the Inbox folder on the Performance>My Forms page, users can now use the Send to Next Step and Send to Previous Step buttons to route up to 5,000 forms at once, a significant increase from the previous limit of 20. Routing results are shown in a dialog or sent by email, depending on the situation.

Enablement: Automatically on

6- Evaluate Next Role Skills and Competencies in Latest Performance Management Forms:

With the latest Performance Management form experience, you can now configure a new type of section that allows users to evaluate the skills or competencies required for an employee's next job role in their career path. You can also use business rules to control the visibility of this section, displaying it only when specific conditions are met.

In the Manage Templates admin tool, after you add an Attribute section (either a skill or competency section) and select Job Specific, you will see a new dropdown field with two options: Current Job Role and Next Job Role. If you choose Next Job Role, the skills or competencies associated with an employee's next role will be auto populated into this section when the form is created.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • Career Path is enabled in Provisioning.
  • You've created career paths that include next job roles.
  • You've enabled the latest experience of Performance Management forms.
  • You've configured a job-specific skill or competency section for the next job role in the form template.
  • If you want to display the next-role section based on conditions, make sure you've configured a business rule for this section using the Configure Dynamic Form Content rule scenario.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • For next-role skills or competencies to appear on the form, the user who creates the form must have the User Permissions>Miscellaneous Permissions>Career Path>View permission for the employee's career path that contains the next role.
  • As in other skill and competency sections, users need permission to view items for the next role:
    • If you have migrated to Talent Intelligence Hub, users need the User Permissions>Growth Portfolio>View permission for Attribute, Attribute Type, Growth Portfolio, Proficiency Level Scale, and Tag.
    • If you have not migrated to Talent Intelligence Hub, and the Competency object is set to secured in the Configure Object Definitions admin tool, users need the User Permissions>Miscellaneous Permissions>Competency permission.

7- Hide Extra Overall Score Displays on Latest Performance Management Forms:

With the latest experience of Performance Management forms, you can now choose to show the overall score only as a clickable value in the form header, hiding it from the Overall Score popover and the end of the form. This helps avoid repetitive information when your form has only one summary section.

By default, when the Overall Score feature is available, users can view the score in three places:

  • As the clickable Overall Score value in the form header
  • At the end of the Overall Score popover
  • At the end of the form

Now, you can hide the score from the last two places by enabling the setting Hide Additional Overall Score Information in the Form Template Settings admin tool.

Enablement: Customer Configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • You've enabled the latest experience of Performance Management forms.
  • To use this feature, make sure you've selected Hide Additional Overall Score Information in the Form Template Settings admin tool.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

You have the Administrator>Manage Templates>Form Templates permission.

8- Latest Experience of Performance Management Forms Automatically On by Default:

The new Performance Management form experience is now automatically enabled in all systems, except those that use the legacy Goal Management or Job Description Manager. If needed, administrators can switch back to the legacy experience.

Enablement: Automatically on

9- Latest Experience of Team Overview in Performance Management:

The latest Team Overview provides a cleaner, more accessible user interface with a template-based view and enhanced navigation, while retaining functionalities of the legacy experience.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

You've selected Enable the latest experience of Team Overview option in the Performance Management Feature Settings admin tool.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

To access Team Overview, users must have the User Permissions>Performance>Team Overview Access permission.

10- Latest Experience of Team Overview in Performance Management:

You can now allow users to manually rate competencies, in addition to rating behaviors, in the latest experience of Performance Management forms.

Previously, when you enabled Rating on behavior for a competency section in the Manage Templates admin tool, users could only rate the behaviors mapped to each competency. The competency ratings were automatically calculated based on the behavior ratings and couldn't be changed by users.

Now, when you enable Rating on behavior, you can further enable Use manual competency rating instead of behavior rating calculation (only applicable to the latest form experience). When enabled, users can manually rate both competencies and their mapped behaviors. Competency ratings must be entered manually and are no longer calculated from behavior ratings. Behavior ratings are for reference only.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • You've enabled the latest experience of Performance Management forms.
  • You've enabled Rating on behavior in the Manage Templates admin tool.
  • To use the new feature, make sure you've enabled Use manual competency rating instead of behavior rating calculation (only applicable to the latest form experience) in the Manage Templates admin tool. This setting is disabled by default.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

You have the Administrator>Manage Templates>Form Templates and Comprehensive template configuration for PMv12 permissions.

11- Latest Experience of Team Overview in Performance Management:

A new admin tool, Mass Change Goal Plan States, is now available for you to change performance goal plan states for multiple employees at once, without needing to open each employee's goal plan individually.

The new tool provides a centralized way to change multiple employees' goal plans to a new state in bulk. It supports uploading a CSV file with up to 30,000 state changes and includes system validation to ensure only valid changes are applied. To handle large volumes efficiently, the system processes the changes in a background job so your other work remains unaffected. You can check the job's progress with the job Mass Change Goal Plan States in Scheduled Job Manager. After the job is completed, you'll receive an email summary so you can easily review the results of the mass change.

Access to this tool is managed through a new role-based permission named Mass Change Goal Plan States.

Enablement: Automatically on

Configuration Requirements:

The latest Goal Management is enabled.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

You have the Administrator Permissions>Manage Goals>Mass Change Goal Plan States permission.

12- New Rule Scenario: Add Custom Validations to Form Fields:

When the latest experience of Performance Management forms is enabled, you can configure business rules that display warning or error messages on forms when certain fields meet the conditions you defined.

Here are some examples of applying this rule scenario:

  • If the manager gives the highest score for more than 75% of an employee's goals, there will be a warning message suggesting reassessment. The manager can choose to adjust their ratings or ignore the warning.
  • If the second-level manager rates a skill lower than the direct manager, a warning message will be displayed, advising them to match or exceed the direct manager's rating.
  • If the HR manager gives an overall potential rating below 4 while selecting the "Ready for Promotion" checkbox, there will be an error message that highlights this discrepancy, requiring revision before submission.

The warning and error messages are created in Manage Data and then associated with specific rule conditions in Configure Business Rules. When forms are saved or being sent to the next step, and the conditions are satisfied, users can see relevant messages by opening the validation dialog from the form footer. All messages generated by rules are grouped under the categories Pay attention to {n} issues found in the form content. and Solve {n} issues found in the form content., as shown in the following screenshot:

This new rule scenario allows you to apply additional validation logic to the form, ensuring a more flexible and thorough review process that meets your organization's requirements.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • You've selected the following options in Performance Management Feature Settings:
    • Enable the latest experience of Performance Management forms
    • Enable business rules for Performance Management
  • You've created a Message Definition object that contains the message to be displayed.
  • You've used the Add Custom Validations to Form Fields rule scenario to create a business rule for the intended form template.
  • You've assigned the rule to the Form Saving trigger.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • Administrator Permissions>Metadata Framework>Manage Data
  • Administrator Permissions>Manage System Properties>Performance Management Feature Settings
  • Administrator Permissions>Metadata Framework>Configure Business Rules
  • Administrator Permissions>Assign Rules in Talent>Assign Rules for Performance Management

13- New Rule Scenario: Launch Form Based on Assessment Outcome:

When the latest experience of Performance Management forms is enabled, you can configure business rules to automatically launch follow-up forms, such as performance improvement plans, when initial forms are submitted with certain ratings. The rules can also inform users about the conditional form launches and carry some information from the initial forms into the follow-up forms.

The new rule scenario, Launch Form Based on Assessment Outcome, involves two types of forms: the initial form and the follow-up form. When the initial form is routed to the next step and its ratings meet the criteria defined in your rule, a follow-up form is automatically created for the same employee. This process is recorded in the audit trail of the follow-up form, with the following system comment: Automatically launched when {username} completes the "{stepName}" step of the "{formTitle}" form (ID: {formId}).

Here are some examples of applying this rule scenario:

  • If the potential rating is "High", launch a leadership development form.
  • If the overall competency rating is "Below Expectations", launch an individual development plan form.
  • If the overall performance rating is "Below Expectations", launch a performance improvement plan form.

In addition to configuring an automatic launch, you can let users know about it by displaying a custom message in the confirmation dialog for sending the form to the next step. You can see an example message in the following screenshot, which reads: "If you send this form with a potential rating of 'High', a leadership development form will be created for this employee." The message is created in Manage Data and then configured in the rule.

You can also use the Get Assessment Summary rule function to specify which information from the initial form will appear on the follow-up form. This provides reviewers with relevant details from the original assessment to support the follow-up process. The new form can display the following data in a predefined text area field: individual goal, skill, and competency ratings; overall ratings; and custom fields in the custom section.

The following screenshot shows a custom section on a follow-up form, with a text area field named "Annual Assessment Results". The field content comes from the initial form, including two competency ratings, an overall performance rating, and content from a custom text area.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • You've selected the following options in Performance Management Feature Settings:
    • Enable the latest experience of Performance Management forms
    • Enable business rules for Performance Management
  • You've configured form templates for the initial form and the follow-up form.
  • (Optional) If you want to carry information from the initial form into the follow-up form, make sure the follow-up form template is configured with a text area custom field in a custom section to display the data. Also make sure that all roles are granted Read permission for this field so the data won't be edited.
  • (Optional) If you want to display a message about the conditional launch in the confirmation dialog for form routing, make sure you've created a Message Definition object that contains the message.
  • You've used the Launch Form Based on Assessment Outcome rule scenario to create a business rule.
  • You've assigned the rule to the Form Routing to Next Step trigger.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • Administrator Permissions>Manage System Properties>Performance Management Feature Settings
  • Administrator Permissions>Manage Templates>Form Templates and Comprehensive template configuration for PMv12
  • Administrator Permissions>Metadata Framework>Configure Business Rules
  • Administrator Permissions>Assign Rules in Talent>Assign Rules for Performance Management

14- Template-Level Permission Control for the Route Form Tool:

As a Role-Based Permissions (RBP) administrator, you can now control which forms are accessible in the Route Form admin tool by applying the Route Document permission to specific form templates. This enhancement is available in Performance Management, 360 Reviews, and Compensation.

In the Manage Permission Roles admin tool, under Administrator Permissions>Manage Documents>Route Document, you can now use a setting to specify which form templates this permission applies to. If this permission is already granted to a role, the setting defaults to All, so the role can continue to view and route forms from all templates using Route Form. If you restrict this permission to specific templates, the role can only access these templates and forms created from them.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

If you've already added Route Document permission to any permission roles, please note that it will be temporarily removed after the Preview or Production release on Friday. It will be automatically restored over the weekend. If you need this permission during the interim period, you can manually add it back so related tasks can be performed.

15- Writing Assistant and Legal Scan Available in Latest Performance Management Forms:

Writing Assistant and Legal Scan, two existing features in the legacy experience of Performance Management forms, are now available in the latest experience as well.

With proper configurations, users can now access Writing Assistant and Legal Scan on the latest form.

Enablement: Customer configured

Configuration Requirements:

  • You've selected Enable the latest experience of Performance Management forms in the Performance Management Feature Settings admin tool.
  • To use Writing Assistant, make sure:
    • Writing Assistant is selected in Provisioning>Company Settings.
    • Enable Writing Assistant is selected in the Form Template Settings admin tool.
    • Writing Assistant is selected in the Custom Elements dialog in the Manage Templates admin tool, if you want to enable this feature for competency custom fields.
  • To use Legal Scan, make sure:
    • Legal Scan is selected in Provisioning>Company Settings.
    • Enable Legal Scan is selected in the Form Template Settings admin tool.
    • Disable Legal Scan on Route is not selected in the Form Template Settings admin tool, if you want Legal Scan to run automatically when forms proceed along the steps.

Role-Based Permission Prerequisites:

  • You have the Administrator>Manage System Properties>Performance Management Feature Settings permission.
  • You have the Administrator>Manage Templates>Form Templates and Comprehensive template configuration for PMv12 permissions.

For a detailed release summary, check out the “What’s New Viewer” for a list of enhancements across the application.

entry.title: Re: What Is Agentic AI and Why SAP Teams Are Paying Attention in 2026
entry.content:
Thanks @Fabio-Martins @luis_almada @Denver_Fletcher91 and good to hear from you all and your insights really motivates me to write more blogs! Great question @Denver_Fletcher91 and you’re right to frame it from a risk and governance perspective.

Most agentic AI systems today are still LLM‑based at their core, but they are not “just an LLM” acting freely. Architecturally, they are better understood as LLMs embedded within a control framework rather than standalone decision-makers.

A typical agentic setup looks something like this:

  • LLM as the reasoning component (interpret intent, plan steps, generate options)
  • Deterministic orchestration layer (rules, policies, workflows, state machines)
  • Tool/action interfaces with explicit constraints (APIs, transactions, approvals)
  • Observability and guardrails (logging, validation, human-in-the-loop where needed)

So yes—the reasoning engine is probabilistic. That implies there will never be mathematical “perfection,” even with clean data and well-defined processes. Agentic systems acknowledge this explicitly rather than pretending otherwise.

Where risk is managed is not by eliminating ambiguity, but by bounding where ambiguity is allowed to exist:

  • The agent can propose actions, but execution is constrained by policy and authorization.
  • The action space is finite and pre-approved, even if the choice among them isn’t deterministic.
  • Confidence thresholds, schema validation, and runtime checks reduce unpredictable outputs.
  • For high-impact decisions, escalation paths (human review, dual control) are mandated.

So the governance question shifts from:

“Can the AI ever make an unexpected decision?”

to:

“What decisions is the AI allowed to make autonomously, under what conditions, and with what auditability?”

In enterprise contexts (including SAP use cases), agentic AI is deliberately designed to separate cognition from control. The LLM reasons; the system governs.

Your point about “windows of opportunity” is absolutely valid—that’s exactly why agentic architectures emphasize policy-driven orchestration, traceability, and reversible actions rather than unconstrained autonomy.

In short:

  • ✅ Yes, LLMs are foundational
  • ❌ No, they are not deployed as unconstrained actors
  • ✅ Governance is achieved through architectural controls, not blind trust in the model

That statement you described isn’t a flaw—it’s the reason agentic AI is being engineered as a system, not a model.

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud CDS of Delivery & Warehouse Order & Warehouse task linkage
entry.content:
Dear SAP Expert,

We would adopt WMS in our business design. Therefore, after DN is created, warehouse task needs to be created by outbound delivery order. However, I have checked the CDS view of I_EWM_WAREHOUSETASK_2 & I_EWM_WAREHOUSEORDER_2 & I_DELIVERYDOCUMENT that there is no linkage found among Outbound delivery, Warehouse order and Warehouse task.

Please advise the CDS view with fields which contains the linkage for Outbound delivery, Outbound delivery order, warehouse order and warehouse task. 

Thank you!

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud CDS of Delivery & Warehouse Order &...
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there is no single CDS view that provides a complete linkage between Outbound Delivery, Outbound Delivery Order, Warehouse Task, and Warehouse Order.

This is by design in embedded EWM. The linkage exists, but it is indirect and must be built step-by-step across different CDS views using key fields.

The relationship works like this:

Outbound Delivery (ERP)
→ Outbound Delivery Order (EWM)
→ Warehouse Task (WT)
→ Warehouse Order (WO)

establish the linkage, you can combine multiple CDS views as follows:

  • Use I_DeliveryDocument to retrieve the Outbound Delivery
  • Use I_EWM_OutbDelivOrdHdr to access the corresponding Outbound Delivery Order
  • Use I_EWM_WarehouseTask_2 to retrieve Warehouse Tasks, which include the reference to the Outbound Delivery Order
  • Use I_EWM_WarehouseOrder_2 to retrieve Warehouse Orders, which are linked via the Warehouse Task

The key relationships are:

  • Outbound Delivery → Outbound Delivery Order
  • Outbound Delivery Order → Warehouse Task
  • Warehouse Task → Warehouse Order

Therefore, the full linkage can be achieved by joining these CDS views using the respective key fields (such as Delivery Document, EWM Outbound Delivery Order, and Warehouse Task/Warehouse Order).

For further reference, please check the documents below:

  • Warehouse Outbound Delivery Order Header Warehouse Outbound Delivery Order Header (I_EWM_OutbDelivOrdHdr)
  • Warehouse Outbound Delivery Order Item Warehouse Outbound Delivery Order Item (I_EWM_OutbDelivOrdItem)
  • Open and Confirmed Warehouse Tasks Open and Confirmed Warehouse Tasks (I_EWM_WarehouseTask_2)
  • Warehouse Task - Query Warehouse Task - Query (C_EWM_WarehouseTaskLeanQ_2)

See Also:

Create a Custom CDS View | SAP Help Portal

Custom CDS Views | SAP Help Portal

 

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: TDS Configuration and deduction under Section 194C
entry.content:
Hi experts!

I’m configuring India TDS 194C (contractor payments) in S/4HANA Extended Withholding Tax and need guidance on the annual aggregate threshold behavior.

Requirement (194C thresholds):

  • Deduct TDS if any single payment/credit exceeds ₹30,000, or
  • Deduct TDS if aggregate payments/credits to the same contractor in a financial year exceed ₹1,00,000.

What I’ve done / issue:

  • I can set a minimum amount = ₹30,000 in the WHT code, which correctly triggers TDS on a single invoice of ₹35,000.
  • However, this minimum-per-document setting seems to prevent TDS from being calculated on smaller invoices even after the vendor’s FY cumulative crosses ₹1,00,000, and I can’t find where to configure the ₹1,00,000 annual accumulation/threshold.

 

Example scenario (each invoice < ₹30,000):

  • Apr: ₹25,000 (cumulative ₹25,000) – no TDS
  • Jun: ₹25,000 (cumulative ₹50,000) – no TDS
  • Aug: ₹25,000 (cumulative ₹75,000) – no TDS
  • Oct: ₹25,000 (cumulative ₹1,00,000) – still no TDS (threshold is “exceeds”)
  • Dec: ₹20,000 (cumulative ₹1,20,000) – TDS should become applicable once the aggregate exceeds ₹1,00,000, but with the ₹30,000 minimum set SAP still doesn’t withhold.

 

Questions:

  1. In standard Extended Withholding Tax, where do we maintain the annual exemption/threshold limit (₹1,00,000) with accumulation?
  2. Can standard config support the “single-sum OR annual-aggregate” logic together (i.e., still withhold on the Dec ₹20,000 once cumulative exceeds ₹1,00,000), or does this require an enhancement/BAdI?

Thanks, happy to share screenshots of our current WHT type/code settings if helpful.

Kat

entry.title: Picking Number Range Configuration by Organizational Unit - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

We would like to request clarification regarding the configuration possibilities for picking document number ranges in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Business Scenario:
Our organization operates multiple sales organizations with different company codes and plants. For internal tracking and audit purposes, the business would like the picking document numbers to reflect the organizational unit that created the document. For example:

  • Picking documents created in one company code should follow a specific number range (e.g., XXXXX–XXXXX).
  • Picking documents created in another company code should follow a different number range (e.g., YYYYY–YYYYY).

Please let us know if it is possible with flexible numbering as well.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Picking Number Range Configuration by Organizational...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

I hope you are doing well.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, number ranges for logistics execution documents, including picking and warehouse-related documents, are not designed to be determined by organizational units such as Company Code, Plant, or Sales Organization. Instead, they are assigned at document type level, and there is no standard configuration or extensibility available to influence this dynamically based on organizational data.

Flexible Numbering is limited to selected business objects and does not cover warehouse or picking documents. In addition, number range settings are restricted to predefined configuration steps (SSCUI), without support for conditional logic based on organizational attributes for this type of document.

In practice, this requirement is usually addressed through alternative approaches. For example, the relevant organizational unit may be captured separately, or the relationship may be handled at reporting level. Standard analytical tools can be used to combine picking documents with delivery and organizational data, such as plant and Company Code, allowing traceability without changing the document numbering logic.

If it is essential for the organizational unit to be reflected directly in the document number, this would require a product enhancement, as this capability is not available in the current standard functionality. In such cases, you may submit an improvement request via the SAP Customer Influence portal, where enhancement requests can be proposed and voted on by other customers.

For guidance, please refer to SAP Note:
3206294- How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management:
https://me.sap.com/notes/3206294/en-US

Regards,
Leticia

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Welcome Session | EMEA | AMER | English
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Have you recently requested provisioning of SAP Cloud ALM? We are thrilled to welcome you aboard! Your journey with #SAP Cloud ALM is about to begin, and we’re here to ensure it is smooth and efficient.

To assist you in this journey, we would like to invite you to one of the below online webinars where we’ll guide you through the setup process and answer any questions you may have.

What to Expect in the Webinar

The SAP Cloud ALM Welcome Webinar has been designed to ensure that you and your team are set up for success. During the session, we will:

  • Demonstrate the initial login procedures to access your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.
  • Provide guidance on the next steps to use the solution effectively.
  • Highlight essential training resources to support you and your team’s learning journey.

Your Next Steps

entry.title: Re: Create new user
entry.content:
I understand you could not create new S-user for *

Regarding to your issue, please kindly note that only user admin/cloud admin/super admin could create S-user.

Since your S-user S0026121735 is not an admin user, you do not have authorizations to create new S-user for others.

In SAP for Me, the My Important Contacts page will display a list of the Super Administrators, Cloud Administrators, and User Administrators for your customer number.

Please kindly contact your admin to help create a new S-user. If you need admin role also, please contact your super admin to assign relevant authorizations to your S-user.

Relevant KBAs:

entry.title: Re: Add metadata in SAP BPA Mail Body
entry.content:
Hello @RomnickJohn ,

You can’t append content directly within the send mail activity in BPA. Instead, use a script task to append the information, then map the resulting URL or content to the email—this approach will work.

entry.title: Re: TDS Configuration and deduction under Section 194C
entry.content:
Answer to your questions:

  1. Annual exemption/threshold limit (₹1,00,000) cannot be maintained in standard config; only per-document minimum is available.
  2. Standard cannot support “single-sum OR annual-aggregate” together; you need an enhancement (via BAdI/user exit) to implement the cumulative threshold logic. You can try with - BAdI FIBL_WT_TAX_CALC or BADI_J_1IEWT_ACCUMULATION which are commonly used to implement cumulative threshold checks for Indian TDS.

entry.title: Re: Automatic Account Determination of OBYC - GBB (VAX/VA...
entry.content:
Why you see only VAY is because, in Public Cloud, if you are using MM-based intercompany procurement, the system posts the payable side (VAY). VAX is tied to SD-based intercompany sales (billing documents). If you haven’t executed an intercompany billing (IV invoice), VAX won’t trigger.

Configure valuation classes/account modifiers in OBYC to differentiate intercompany postings.

  • VAX = selling company revenue (SD intercompany billing).
  • VAY = buying company payable (MM intercompany procurement).

In Public Cloud, you will see VAY more often because IC procurement is MM-driven, not SD-driven.

entry.title: Re: How to connect to SAP HANA using VSCode SQL Tool Sett...
entry.content:
Guide: Connecting SAP HANA to VS Code via SQLTools

Integrating your SAP HANA database with Visual Studio Code allows for a seamless development experience. Follow this structured guide to configure your connection using the SQLTools extension and the SAP HANA Driver.

Prerequisites

Before starting, ensure you have the SQLTools extension installed in VS Code.

Step 1: Provision SAP HANA Service Instance

To establish a connection, you must first generate the necessary credentials within the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) cockpit.

  1. Navigate to your Subaccount and go to Instances and Subscriptions.

  2. Create a new instance of SAP HANA Schemas & HDI Containers.

  3. Once the instance is created, generate a Service Key.

  4. Open the Service Key and locate the following parameters:

    • host

    • port (typically 443)

    • user (or hdi_user)

    • password (or hdi_password)

      Step 2: Install the SAP HANA Driver

      To enable HANA support within SQLTools, you must install the specific driver:

      1. Open the VS Code Marketplace.

      2. Search for and install the SAP HANA Driver for SQLTools.

      Step 3: Configure the Connection Assistant

      Once the driver is active, you can link your database through the SQLTools interface.

      1. Open the SQLTools icon in the activity bar and click on Add New Connection.

      2. Select the SAP HANA tile to launch the Connection Assistant.

      3. Fill in the following fields using the values from your Service Key:

      4. FieldValue / Description
        Connection nameA unique identifier for your project.
        HANA ServerEnter the host value from your Service Key.
        Server PortEnter 443.
        SchemaEnter the hdi_user value (standard HDI schemas match the username).
        UsernameEnter the hdi_user value.
        PasswordEnter the hdi_password value.

        Step 4: Validate and Connect

        1. Click Test Connection to ensure the credentials and network path are valid.

        2. If successful, click Save Connection.

        3. Locate your new connection in the sidebar and click Connect.

          Results

          Upon a successful connection, the SQLTools sidebar will populate with your database catalog, allowing you to browse tables, views, and metadata. A Session SQL tab will open automatically, enabling you to execute queries and perform local data testing directly within VS Code.

entry.title: What’s new in Mobile development kit client 26.3
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I'm happy to announce that a new release of the Mobile Development Kit (MDK) is now available for all SAP Build and Mobile Services customers. You can download it from the SAP Software Center, and it will soon be available on the community download page.

The SAP Mobile Services Client has been updated to version 26.3.0 on the Apple App Store and the Google Play Store.

SAP MDK enables developers and technical business users to build cross-platform mobile applications. It allows you to build your application once, in an integrated development environment (SAP Business Application Studio / Visual Studio Code) and run it natively on Mobile (iOS & Android).  

Below UI enhancements are available only on iOS and Android platforms:

  • Support Styling of Key Value Item Control
  • Contact Cell Control Enhancements
  • Support In-App Camera in FormCell Attachment Control (Android Only)
  • New ActionBar Caption Size Options
  • Support Required Field Indicator for Segmented FormCell Control
  • Support Uploading Offline Store to Mobile Services
  • Onboarding via MDM App Config (Managed Configuration)

Support Styling of Key Value Item Control

MDK now supports styling Key Value item control. A new object property Styles has been introduced with following sub-properties:

  • KeyValueItem: supports only background color to style entire item.
  • KeyName: supports to style key name.
  • Value: supports to style a value property.
//excerpt of an MDK page
"KeyAndValues": [{
        "Value": "{City}",
        "_Type": "KeyValue.Type.Item",
        "_Name": "KeyValue0",
        "KeyName": "City",
        "Visible": true,
        "OnPress": "/MDK263/Actions/UpdateEntitySuccessMessage.action",
        "LinkColor": "#0000FF",
        "Styles": {
            "KeyValueItem": "KeyValueItemBackground",
            "KeyName": "KeyNameStyle",
            "Value": "KeyValueStyle"
        }
    },

//excerpt of a .less file 
.KeyValueItemBackground {
    background-color: #FFA500;
}
.KeyNameStyle {
    font-color: #ADD8E6;
    text-decoration: underline;
}
.KeyValueStyle {
    font-color: green;
}

Note:  : Value  style will take priority over LinkColor, regardless of whether OnPress is defined or not.

 

Key Value Styling on iOS and Andrid devices

 For more information, refer to the Key Value Item documentation.

Contact Cell Control Enhancements

  • Support for Initials: The ContactCell component now supports displaying user initials, providing a more personalized and recognizable visual experience when profile pictures are unavailable. This enhancement makes it easier to scan and identify contacts by replacing generic placeholder icons with meaningful initials. Three new properties have been introduced to support this feature:
    • DetailImageText: A string property that displays text in the image container when no DetailImage is present. It supports a maximum of 2–4 characters, allowing you to manually specify custom text such as initials.
    • DetailImageIsCircular: A boolean property with a default value of true that controls the shape of the image container. When set to true, the image is displayed in a circular shape; when set to false, it is displayed in a rounded rectangular shape.
    • UseHeadlineForDetailImage: A boolean property with a default value of false. When set to true and no DetailImage is provided, the control automatically extracts and displays the first two initials from the Headline property in the image container. This property takes precedence over DetailImageText.
  • Support for BadgeImage Property:  A new property, BadgeImage, is now available on ContactCell. This property allows you to attach an additional indicator icon or image to the existing DetailImage or DetailImageText for each contact. For example, it can be used to highlight and identify primary contacts in a contact list. Additionally, the existing Styles property for ContactCellItem now supports a new subproperty named BadgeImage, which enables CSS styling for the indicator icon or image defined in the BadgeImage property.
//excerpt of .page
{
    "ContactCell": {
        "_Name": "ContactCellItem6",
        "BadgeImage": "sap-icon://bell",
        "DetailImageText": "SB",
        "DetailImageIsCircular": true,
        "Headline": "Sue Bay",
        "Subheadline": "Antioch, Illinois",
        "UseHeadlineForDetailImage": false,
        "Visible": true,
        "ActivityItems": [{
                "_Name": "ContactCellItem4ActivityItems0",
                "ActivityType": "Phone"
            }
        ],
        "Styles": {
            "DetailImage": "DetailImage",
            "BadgeImage": "BadgeImage"
        }
    }
},

//excerpt of Styles.less
.BadgeImage {
    color: #068831;
    background-color: #ffae00;
}
.DetailImage {
    color: #AD00FF;
    background-color: #ADD8E6;
}

Contact Cell Control on iOS and Android devices

For more information, refer to the Contact Cell documentation.

Support In-App Camera in FormCell Attachment Control (Android Only)

We have received feedback from customers about the MDK app restarting after taking a photo using the Attachment control, especially when the app is running on Android devices with lower RAM. This may be due to memory pressure on the device, which can cause the OS to kill the MDK app running on background after launching the camera.

To address this problem, we are providing support for using an in-app camera option. This option consumes less memory, and the camera opens directly inside the MDK app instead of switching to a separate camera application.

  • A new UseInAppCamera property is available in the Application.app file under the Settings object. It determines whether the in-app camera or the device's default camera will be used for the AttachmentFormCell control’s “Take Photo” option.
    • When set to true, the in-app camera will be used instead of launching the device’s native camera application.
//excerpt of Application.app
{
    "MainPage": "/MDK263/Pages/Main.page",
    "OnLaunch": [
        "/MDK263/Rules/Service/Initialize.js"
    ],
    "Settings": {
        "UseInAppCamera": true
    },
    "_Name": "MDK263"
}

In addition, we are introducing two new APIs to manage the in-app camera settings programmatically:

  • getUseInAppCamera 
    • Retrieves the current state of the in-app camera usage flag.
    • Returns the current value determining whether the in-app camera is enabled for FormCell Attachment controls.
  • setUseInAppCamera 
    • Enables or disables the in-app camera for FormCell Attachment controls
    • Changes take effect immediately for new AttachmentFormCell instances
    • For AttachmentFormCell controls that are already rendered on the current page, the new setting will only take effect after calling the control’s reset() API. Calling the reset() API will discard any existing attachments in the control.
export default function ChangeUseInAppCamera(context) {
  let currentValue = context.getUseInAppCamera();
  context.setUseInAppCamera(!currentValue);
  alert('UseInAppCamera set to ' + !currentValue);
  let currentPageProxy = context.getPageProxy();
  if (currentPageProxy) {
    if (currentPageProxy.getName() === 'FCMain') {
      let attachmentControlProxy = context.evaluateTargetPathForAPI('#Page:FCMain/#Control:FormCellAttachment0');
      if (attachmentControlProxy) {
        attachmentControlProxy.reset();
      }
    }
  }
}

New ActionBar Caption Size Options

The ActionBar now supports flexible caption sizing through the new PreferredCaptionSize property, which replaces the existing PrefersLargeCaption property.

  • Enhanced size control: Choose from small, medium, or large caption sizes instead of toggling between only two options.
    • iOS supports small and large caption size
    • Android supports small, medium, and large caption size.

Behavior Notes:        

  • When PreferredCaptionSize is set to "large", any defined Subhead will be ignored.
  • When setting CaptionAlignment (Android only) to “center”, the PreferCaptionSize automatically set to small.  
  • On iOS, large caption mode is not supported on pages with BottomNavigation, Tabs, SideDrawer, FormCellContainer, or FlexibleColumn controls.
  • If both PreferredCaptionSize and the deprecated PrefersLargeCaption property are specified, PreferredCaptionSize takes precedence.
//excerpt of an MDK page 
"ActionBar": {
        "Items": [
            {
                "_Type": "Control.Type.ActionBarItem",
                "_Name": "ActionBarItem0",
                "Caption": "User Menu",
                "OnPress": "/MDK263/Actions/Application/UserMenuPopover.action"
            }
        ],
        "_Name": "ActionBar1",
        "_Type": "Control.Type.ActionBar",
        "Caption": "MDK 26.3",
        "PreferredCaptionSize": "Medium"
    }
}

 

Support Required Field Indicator  for Segmented FormCell Control 

In MDK 25.9 release, we introduced support for required field indicators for some FormCell controls. In this release, we are extending this support to the Segmented FormCell, indicating that the field must be filled out.
Note: This feature does not enforce users to fill out the form cell; it only provides a visual indicator that the field is required.

//Excerpt from a .page
{
    "RequiredIndicator": true,
    "_Type": "Control.Type.FormCell.SegmentedControl",
    "_Name": "PrioritySegmented",
    "Caption": "Priority",
    "ApportionsSegmentWidthsByContent": false,
    "Segments": [
        "Low",
        "Medium",
        "High"
    ]
}

Form Cell Segmented control on iOS and Android devices

 

Support Uploading Offline Store to Mobile Services 

MDK now provides the ability for applications to upload the offline store (Both the main offline database (UDB) and the Request Queue database) to Mobile Services to assist with troubleshooting. This capability helps developers and support teams more effectively investigate and diagnose issues related to the offline store, reducing the time required to identify the root cause of problems reported by business users.
To facilitate this capability, a new MDK action has been introduced: Action.Type.OfflineOData.UploadStore.

{
    "_Type": "Action.Type.OfflineOData.UploadStore",
    "ActionResult": {
        "_Name": "UploadOfflineDB"
    },
    "Service": "/MDK263/Services/SampleServiceV4.service",
    "EncryptionKey": "MDK123", // encryption key used during the upload process
    "Note": "My Test App" // Optional note attached to the uploaded database file
}

In order to use this feature, you will need to first enable offline store upload policy in SAP Mobile Services Admin UI for a given MDK app. Developers can download the uploaded files on their local machine and use ILODATA tool (link to doc) to analyze them.  (link how to use this tool). For more information, see this  documentation.

Onboarding via MDM App Config (Managed Configuration)

MDK now supports onboarding via MDM App Config (Managed Configuration). Company administrators can push onboarding settings directly to managed devices through their MDM solution.

The MDK client retrieves MDK-defined configurations from a reserved root property named MDKSettings within the Managed Configuration. Currently, this root property only accepts the ConnectionSettings object. The supported properties, their functionality, and the required structure for ConnectionSettings remain the same as in the existing implementation.

While the iOS and Android versions differ syntactically due to their file formats, they share the same structural hierarchy. Note that the actual syntax may vary depending on the requirements of the specific MDM solution being used.

  • iOS: MDM solutions for iOS commonly use an XML format for complex configurations.
  • Android: MDM solutions for Android commonly use a JSON format for complex configurations.

Become an SAP MDK Explorer!
Do you have a requirement to build a mobile solution for your enterprise? Take your first steps today by following these tutorials to get started and learn more about the SAP Mobile development kit. We’re excited to see what you build—your feedback and ideas are invaluable!

Submit Improvement Requests
You can submit your improvement request (link) for Mobile development kit to the SAP Customer Influence site, a central place for all product improvement requests. You can browse all improvement requests, submit improvement requests, comment, vote, receive updates, and see who has voted at the SAP Customer Influence site.

entry.title: Re: Temporary Protected Status Extension
entry.content:
@jphaney , this could be recorded during Form I-9 Section 1 in the event that the New Hire Document Upload control is enabled. The New Hire has an opportunity to enter the appropriate Expiration Date here.

In addition, during Section 2 (and possibly 3), the proper Expiration Date can be entered. There is also a text field for the "Additional Notes" which is where any Notes related to this Form I-9 would be recorded.

entry.title: Re: SLED mandatory check ignored in Fiori App F1043 (Post...
entry.content:
Why the behavior differs is because

MIGO (GUI) fully respects the movement type setting (“1 = Enter Expiration Date”) and the material master’s shelf life fields. With Total Shelf Life = 0 and Min. Rem. Shelf Life > 0, the system forces manual entry of the SLED.

In Fiori F1043, the app is built on newer APIs and does not enforce the same mandatory check when Total Shelf Life = 0. It only makes the field mandatory if a production date is entered or if total shelf life is maintained. As a result, users can bypass the SLED entry.

Having said that your customizing is correct; the discrepancy is due to the Fiori app’s current logic. To force SLED mandatory in F1043, you need an enhancement (BAdI or app extension).

entry.title: SAP Service Request FAQs
entry.content:
The Service Requests application offers the ability to request services for delivered systems, based on the SAP Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS)  

Roles and Responsibilities (R&R) document. The service request catalogue includes services categorized as: 

 Standard services: non-chargeable and provided as part of the standard contract.  
Optional services: Subject to additional service fees and require a contractual change request.  
Chargeable services:  

  • Enhanced Managed Services (EMS): Supplemental set of services subject to additional fees for SAP HANA Enterprise Cloud (BYOL and Subscription) contracts, which could or could not be performed by customer.  
  • Additional Services: Supplemental set of services subject to additional fees for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, and SAP HANA Enterprise Cloud Advanced Edition contracts. 

 Access the application via SAP for Me > ECS Workspace > Click Create Service Requests   

 

Steps: 

1.Logon SAP for Me > Service & Support dashboard 

2.Go to Service Request tab https://me.sap.com/servicessupport/servicerequests 

3.Click on 'New Service Request' tab  

4.Select desired template >> Proceed to create 

5.Authorizations:  

6.Create Billable Service Requests     

  • Create Service Requests 
  • Display Service Requests 
  • Report Technical Problem  
  • (for template) - Note: If templates are missing, the authorization needs to be set at the customer number or CCOE level (not just installation number level) - see KBA 3463116. 
  • If unable to create SR or any issues during SR application - create a case for component XX-SER-FORME-ECS with relevant screenshots and details. 

Further details can be found on KBA 2720477 How to access ECS Service Requests - SAP for Me   

https://me.sap.com/notes/3239384/E- How to use Service Requests application - SAP for Me   
https://me.sap.com/notes/3432011/E 
https://me.sap.com/notes/3355416/E 
https://me.sap.com/notes/3462685 

3463116 - Missing template(s) when creating service request 

What is Service Request in SAP for Me? (check 2nd half of the blog) 

 Link for Service request creation: 

https://learning.sap.com/videos/creating-a-service-request-in-sap-for-me 

 You can also check this document that explains the use of Service Request: https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/my-support/help-for-sap-support-applic...    

 Guides: Video   

https://learning.sap.com/videos/using-a-service-request-application-in-sap-for-me 

 Thank you for reading!   

We hope you found this helpful.
